rulings.Case Title: JULIAN WYLER ET AL VS. PJ CON, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 21, 2018, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANTs JOHN NEVAREZ and PJ CON, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT Cross-defendants PJ Con, Inc. and John R. Nevarez's demurrer to amended cross complaint is: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to Mike Jozinovic's cause of action for breach of contract. The allegation that Mike was a contracting party is a legal conclusion and the more specific allegations indicate that only MJ Tile, Marija Jozinovic's sole proprietorship, was the contracting party. (See pars. BC-1, 3, and Ex. A of the Amended Cross-Complaint.) SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to Mike Jozinovic's cause of action for common counts. (See Harris v. Kessler (1932) 124 Cal.App. 299, 303.) SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to Mike Jozinovic's cause of action for fraud. The representations were being made to MJ Tile. The demurrer is OVERRULED as to MJ Tile's claim for fraud. Pars. FR-1-6 allege the claim with sufficient particularity. The misrepresentation occurred on 6/20/16, John Nevarez made the misrepresentation by electronic message, MJ Tile sufficiently alleges scienter and reliance, and less particularity is required where the facts are presumptively within cross-defendants' knowledge. (See Turner v. Milstein (1951) 103 Cal.App.2d 651, 658.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for PJ Con is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: MARTIFER-SILVERADO FUND I, LLC VS. TALESUN SOLAR USA LTD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel A Further Response And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 21, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANTs TALESUN SOLAR USA LTD and ZHONGLI NEW ENERGY USA CO., LLC's Motion To Compel A Further Response And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: There will be no tentative ruling on this motion; parties should appear and be prepared to address their respective positions. Also, counsel for moving party should prepare a proposed order reflecting the relief sought and submit a copy to opposing counsel for review prior to the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MAX C SNYDER VS. PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 21, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Pacific Gas and Electric Company's demurrer to the second amended complaint is OVERRULED. Plaintiff Max Snyder's amended charge to the Department of Fair Employment and Housing relates back to his original DFEH charge. Snyder's original allegations that he was terminated for seeking medical leave are consistent with theories of disability discrimination and failure to accommodate; the amended charge alleged the medical leave was related to Snyder's disability. This is different from Rodriguez v. Airborne Express (9th Cir. 2001) 265 F.3d 890, 899, where the two forms of alleged discrimination were race and disability, not disability and medical leave. Snyder's original charge alleged harassment and retaliation, so such allegations in the amended charge relate back as well. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Snyder is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: FABIO G GIOTTA VS. CAFFE TRIESTE, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Plaintiff Fabio G. Giotta To Strike Or Tax Costs Of Defendant Ida Zoubi
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 21, 2018, Line 7, PLAINTIFF FABIO GIOTTA Motion By Plaintiff Fabio G. Giotta To Strike Or Tax Costs Of Defendant Ida Zoubi If the tentative ruling is contested, the motion will be heard at 10:30am, not 9:30am, by Judge Kahn. Plaintiff Fabio Giotta's motion to strike or tax costs claimed by defendant Ida Zoubi is granted in part. Ms. Zoubi is entitled to costs in the amount of $4,006.54. Ms. Zoubi's opposition papers included a revised costs memorandum. All costs claimed in the original costs memorandum not included in the revised memorandum are taxed based on Ms. Zoubi's concession that she should not recover those costs. In addition, the court taxes two-thirds of the costs for Mr. Giotta's deposition since those costs are properly equally allocable among all three of the clients represented by the Schrerer firm. All other costs claimed on the revised memorandum were reasonably incurred and recoverable by Ms. Zoubi. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Zoubi is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: SAMUEL JACKSON VS. SBS CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Respond To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Documents; To Deem Facts Admitted Or Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admissions, And To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Further Responses To Form Interrogatories; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 21, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT SBS CORPORATION's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Respond To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Documents; To Deem Facts Admitted Or Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admissions, And To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Further Responses To Form Interrogatories; Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant, SBS Corporation's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories nos. 2.13, 8.1, 8.2, 8.4, 8.5, 8.7, 8.8, 9.1, 9.2, 12.2, 12.4, 12.7,14.1, 14.2 and 17.1 is granted. It is denied as to 12.15. Plaintiff is ordered to serve further responses, without objection within fifteen (15) days from the date of this order. Defendant, SBS Corporation's motion to deem facts admitted is denied, its motion to compel further responses is granted in part and denied in part as follows: 1. All plaintiff's objections are overruled. 2. Plaintiff will serve defendant within (15) days from the date of this order, without objection, code compliant responses to requests nos. 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 14, 15, 16 & 17 which either admit, deny or set forth an inability to either admit or deny the facts asserted therein. 3. As to all other requests for admission, the motion is denied. It appears that plaintiff has responded with a denial or inability to admit or deny to all of these and his responses to interrogatory 17.1 are sufficient. 4. Monetary sanctions against plaintiff and his counsel in the amount of $2000.00 which will be paid to defendant and its counsel within ten (10) days from the date of this order. 5. Counsel for defendant will prepare and appropriate order, submit to counsel for plaintiff for approval as to form and deliver a copy to the court at the hearing on December 21, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 21, 2018, Line 9, PLAINTIFF STACEY CURTIS' MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD The Ottinger Firm P.C.'s motion to withdraw is GRANTED. Based on the evidence before the Court, it is clear that the attorney-client relationship has deteriorated and that the Ottinger Firm can no longer effectively represent Plaintiff Stacey Curtis. Counsel is not limited to withdrawing for cause, but may withdraw when it can be accomplished without undue prejudice to client. (Ramirez v. Sturdevant (1994) 21 Cal.App.4th 904, 915.) Because there is no indication that Plaintiff will be prejudiced, it is permissible for the Firm to withdraw from representation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the order submitted with the moving papers. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: JOHN N. STATHAKIS ET AL VS. NICHOLAS D. BOTAITIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Compel Further Response To Interrogatories; Declaration Of William E. King; And For Monetary Sanction Against Defendants In Amount Of 7,860.00
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 21, 2018, Line 5, PLAINTIFFs JOHN STATHAKIS and KRISTOPHER STATHAKIS' Motion To Compel Further Response To Interrogatories; Declaration Of William E. King; And For Monetary Sanction Against Defendants In Amount Of 7,860.00 Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs' motion to compel further responses to certain interrogatories directed to plaintiffs is granted in part and denied in part as follows: 1. Plaintiffs' objections to special interrogatories Nos 2, 6, 12, &21 are overruled. Plaintiffs are ordered to serve within fifteen (15) days from the date of this order further Reponses setting forth the facts, not legal conclusions, which they contend support their contentions. 2. Plaintiffs' objections to special interrogatories Nos 4, 8, 12 & 23 are overruled. Plaintiffs are ordered to serve within fifteen (15) days from the date of this order, further responses identifying with specificity the documents upon which they are relying in support of their contentions. 3. Plaintiffs' objections to special interrogatory no. 9 are overruled. Plaintiffs are ordered to serve within fifteen (15) days from the date of this order a response to interrogatory no 9 with a yes or no answer or an explanation as to why they cannot do so. 4. Plaintiffs' objections as to interrogatories 13, 14, 15, 16 17 & 19 on the grounds of relevancy are sustained. 5. Monetary sanctions of the amount of $2,500.00 will be assessed against plaintiffs and their attorney payable to defendant within ten days from the date of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ABSOLUTE RESOLUTIONS CORPORATION VS. PAULA LONG HAFDAHL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion For Order Deeming Admissions Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 21, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFF ABSOLUTE RESOLUTIONS CORPORATION's Motion For Order Deeming Admissions Admitted Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition having been filed in response to plaintiff absolute resolutions corporation's motion for an order deeming admissions the court orders as follows: 1. Defendant Paula Long Hafdahl will serve on plaintiff full and complete code compliant responses to plaintiff's request for admission within ten (10) days from the date of this order, 2. If defendant fails to comply with the requirements of paragraph (1) above, all of the requests for admission will be deemed to have been admitted and defendant will be precluded from challenging the truth of those admission. No monetary sanctions were sought and none will be imposed. Plaintiff's counsel should prepare and appropriate Oder, submit to defendant for approval as to form and deliver a copy to the court at the hearing on December 21, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GARY CONVERSE VS. KELLY PATEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, To Deem Matters Admitted, Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 21, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT KELLY PATEL's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, To Deem Matters Admitted, Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Although no opposition was filed to defendant's motion to compel discovery responses, to deem matters admitted and for sanctions. The court is compelled to deny the motion without prejudice to it being refiled and re-noticed. The initial notice of motion to compel discovery responses, to deem matters admitted, requests for sanction which was filed on nov.20, 2018 stated that it would "move the court for an order compelling defendant to respond" obviously, from the papers this was an incorrect statement, but where the plaintiff is self-represented, I believe it is incumbent upon the court to require defendant to refile and re-notice with the correct statement of the relief sought. Counsel should prepare an appropriate order, submit a copy to plaintiff for approval as to form and deliver it to the court at the hearing on December 21, 2018 Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KAREN Y UCHIYAMA VS. GLORIA LOPEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 21, 2018, Line 12, CROSS DEFENDANT KAREN UCHIYAMA's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT If the tentative ruling is contested, the motion will be heard at 10:30am, not 9:30am, by Judge Kahn. Cross-defendant Karen Uchiyama's demurrer to all three causes of action in the cross-complaint filed by cross-complainant Gloria Lopez is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Among other possible reasons, the negligence and breach of contract claims are deficient because the delayed discovery allegation fails to allege any facts showing that Ms. Lopez could not "through the use of reasonable diligence" discovered the facts on which those claim are based within the one year of their occurrence to avoid the claims being time-barred by CCP 340.6. Among other possible reasons, the fraud claim is deficient because it fails to allege what acts Ms. Lopez took in reliance on the alleged misrepresentations or why such reliance was reasonable. If she can do so in good faith and her counsel's compliance with CCP 128.7, Ms. Lopez is granted leave to file a first amended cross-complaint correcting these deficiencies. If a first amended cross-complaint is filed and Ms. Uchiyama files another demurrer, Ms. Uchiyama is reminded that a demurrer must solely be based on the words of the challenged pleading and judicially noticeable facts and must not make any arguments based on other purported facts, as this demurrer did repeatedly. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Ms. Uchiyama is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu
